change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew 549% (168→1090 chars) since c2 review on 2026-04-06 — likely post-hoc standard-dataset verdict block was appended; detail_evidence grew 34% (1059→1420) reflecting softened Terra-Nova claims and added LLSVP argument. DOI was fixed (epsl→pepi) per c2 critical hole.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. MHD core dynamics (Terra-Nova et al. 2017) provide the spatial mechanism for SAA bifurcation via reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary. Westward drift ~0.2–0.3°/year is explained by flow-driven advection in a rotating spherical shell; asymmetric decay (African vs American cell) is attributed to the African LLSVP altering local core flow. The dome has no MHD equations and no mechanism for azimuthal asymmetry. Verdict backed by post-hoc trap: CHAOS-7 and Terra-Nova are pre-existing public datasets; dome did not pre-register its 30.8→50.6° bracket.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt — PROS-001 (prospective claim) and WIN-004 CONFIRMED entry
raw-text/08-live-power.txt — 20 Domains list mentions 'SAA decay' (rate), not separation
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The dome's 20-domain monitor automation live-fetches a DECAY RATE (field drop at SAA-region stations, ≥28 nT/year threshold, which maps to WIN-037/042). The separation VALUE (50.57°) appears in 05-model.txt as an architectural constant alongside other static parameters (sun altitude, moon altitude, firmament height). No live-fetch pipeline for angular separation was located; the monitor derives the decay rate and pairs that with the static 50.57° separation value. PRED-013 (120-180° by 2055) is not auto-monitored — it is a 30-year-horizon manual target.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The 50.57° separation value is hardcoded in the dome's architecture file. The dome's 'exponential curve' claim has no specified tau and no code that fits a functional form — it is a qualitative label. Two endpoints (30.8° in 2000, 50.6° in 2025) fit equally well to linear, exponential, quadratic, or logarithmic forms; the dome's 'exponential' assertion is underdetermined.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 geomagnetic field model (Finlay et al. 2020) — official coverage to ~2020
NOAA WMM — possible extension for 2021-2025 (not directly confirmed)
INTERMAGNET station data — for live decay-rate monitoring (different WIN)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: If separation decelerates toward 2055: dome can invoke 'aetheric damping threshold.' If it accelerates: PRED-013 confirmed. If it stays linear (most likely): the dome has no public commitment to what 'exponential' means numerically (no tau published), so it can retroactively recharacterize the curve.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our current code_analysis tag says monitoring='live_fetch' but no live-fetch pipeline for SAA ANGULAR SEPARATION has been located in the dome's raw text. The 20-domain list mentions 'SAA decay' (rate in nT/year) not separation; 05-model.txt hardcodes the 50.57° separation value. WIN-005 (African cell faster decay — same CHAOS-7 dataset, same Terra-Nova citation) is tagged 'hardcoded,' creating an internal inconsistency between two tightly-coupled WINs. Either both should be 'hardcoded' or both 'live_fetch' — the SAA bundle cannot be split that way without explicit evidence of a separation-specific live pipeline.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: 05-model.txt line 43 lists 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg' as an architectural parameter alongside other static model constants. The 20-domain live-fetch list (08-live-power.txt) mentions 'SAA decay' — a rate metric that maps to WIN-037/042, not WIN-004's angular separation. WIN-005 (same data bundle) is already tagged 'hardcoded.' Changing WIN-004 from 'live_fetch' to 'hardcoded' eliminates a cross-WIN inconsistency and matches the verifiable evidence.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: SAA bifurcation is explained by standard geomagnetism as the surface expression of reversed-flux patches at the core-mantle boundary (Terra-Nova et al. 2017, PEPI). The phenomenon is predicted qualitatively by dynamo models; the dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric vortex decay' without changing any predicted value.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020) documented SAA bifurcation in public literature before the dome model incorporated it. PROS-001 claims 'prospective' status but the 30.8°/50.6° bracket was readable from CHAOS-7 snapshots at the time of the dome commit. No timestamped pre-registration in a venue independent of the author was located.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The dome's PROS-001 and WIN-004 text states 'exponential separation' and 'decay curve of the aetheric vortex' but publishes no tau, no functional form, and no derivation from dome geometric parameters (disc radius 20,015 km, firmament height 9,086 km, aether coupling κ). 'Exponential' here is a label, not a derivation.

holes_found.description: Unverified concession in finding field. The summary-table finding reads 'Author concedes method invalid' but no such concession is present in the dome's current raw text (01-home.txt through 08-live-power.txt) or on the evolution page. The evolution page admits earlier versions 'failed to mathematically reconcile SAA splitting' (v0.4) but that is not the same as the author conceding the CURRENT WIN-004 method is invalid. This is the summary text readers see first — if a dome defender challenges the quote and we cannot produce it, the entire WIN entry is discredited. The c2 review flagged the same issue ('station ratio proxy method was methodologically invalid') and the exact phrase was dropped, but the 'concedes method invalid' framing persists in the finding field without a traceable source.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) locate the specific dome source (git commit, changelog, archived version) that contains a clear concession and add a linked citation, or (b) rewrite finding to match what is verifiable. Proposed: 'MHD core dynamics explain SAA bifurcation via reversed-flux patches; dome has no fluid mechanism. Two data points (30.8→50.6°) fit linear as well as exponential.' This is defensible without requiring a concession quote.

holes_found.description: code_analysis tag monitoring='live_fetch' is not supported by the evidence and conflicts with WIN-005. 05-model.txt hardcodes 'SAA separation (2025): 50.57 deg' as a static value. The live-fetch 20-domain list references 'SAA decay' (rate in nT/year) — that maps to WIN-037/042, not angular separation. WIN-005 (African cell faster decay, same dataset) is already tagged 'hardcoded.' Per our CLAUDE.md rule ('Never assume the previous reviewer got it right. WIN-014 was incorrectly tagged as live_fetch...'), this is exactly the failure mode we are supposed to catch.
holes_found.recommendation: Change code_analysis.monitoring to 'hardcoded' and update Part 2b aggregate counts if needed. The decider should apply this tag change and confirm the Part 2b prose ('20/31 hardcoded' or similar) still reflects the corrected tag data.

holes_found.description: Finding text overstates Terra-Nova et al. (2017). The finding says 'MHD core dynamics derive SAA splitting from first principles.' The detail_evidence — now appropriately softened — says the paper 'addresses the SAA's origin and geographic position; it does not specifically derive the inter-cell bifurcation rate.' The finding contradicts its own detail. A dome defender clicking through will spot the internal inconsistency and attack the summary. The same overstatement pattern was flagged in c2 for the detail block and was fixed there, but it survived in the finding field.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite finding to: 'MHD core dynamics explain the SAA bifurcation mechanism (reversed-flux patches at the CMB); dome has no fluid mechanism. The specific bifurcation rate is not first-principles-derived by either model.' This is honest and still damages the dome's 'globe has no mechanism' claim.

holes_found.description: detail_claim field reads 'Globe models have no mechanism for SAA splitting or asymmetric decay' without attribution. This is the dome's position, not ours. Without a 'the dome claims...' framing, a dome defender can misread it as our own concession to the dome's framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Prefix with 'The dome model asserts that' or move the globe-no-mechanism claim into quotes. Minor but preempts a rhetorical gotcha.

holes_found.description: Our c2 review's 'stronger argument' contained an arithmetic error: '50.6 + 0.8*25 = 70.6 deg' by 2055. The correct span is 2055-2025 = 30 years, giving 50.6 + 0.8*30 = 74.4°. If this number ever gets integrated into the published text unchanged, a dome defender will catch the off-by-one. Flagging so the decider does not propagate it.
holes_found.recommendation: If/when 2055 linear extrapolation is added to the published text, use 74.4° (or 74.36° if strict with rate = 0.792 deg/year), and state 'required rate to reach 120° by 2055 is 2.31°/year, 2.92× the current rate; to reach 180° is 4.31°/year, 5.45× current.'

stronger_arguments.description: PREDICTION CONTENT TEST. The dome's 'exponential' label has zero information content without a published tau. Our text should state this plainly: 'With only two endpoints (2000, 2025), any monotonic increasing function fits exactly. The dome specifies no functional form beyond the word exponential and no tau — so it cannot be distinguished from linear, quadratic, or logarithmic curves by future data until ~2035. The prediction becomes falsifiable only when the dome commits to a tau.' This converts the 'globe has no mechanism' rhetorical win into a demand the dome cannot meet without abandoning its flexibility.
sources: dome raw-text/02-wins.txt PROS-001 (no tau specified); raw-text/03-predictions.txt PRED-013

stronger_arguments.description: PRED-013 FEASIBILITY MATH. 50.6° in 2025 → 120–180° by 2055 requires 2.31–4.31°/year, i.e. 2.9–5.5× the current ~0.8°/year rate. Neither model currently observes an acceleration of this magnitude. Adding this calculation to the WIN-004 panel — with explicit arithmetic — credits the dome for making a testable long-horizon claim while showing that the observed rate has NOT started accelerating toward PRED-013's band. If a dome defender points to PRED-013 as a bold prospective claim, this math shows the boldness is numerical, not physical.

LLSVP AZIMUTHAL ASYMMETRY IS DISCRIMINATORY. Our detail already mentions the African LLSVP. This is the strongest discriminator between models — the dome's claim of a 'radially symmetric aetheric vortex' is incompatible with the African-American asymmetry. A radially symmetric mechanism cannot produce azimuthal structure; observed asymmetry is a clean falsification of the dome's geometry. We should lift this from a single sentence in detail_evidence into the summary-table finding.
sources: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) PEPI; LLSVP literature (Garnero & McNamara 2008)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies SAA bifurcation as a real, scientifically interesting phenomenon. It also flags that MHD papers like Terra-Nova et al. do not publish first-principles predictions of the specific 30.8°→50.6° bifurcation rate — that gap is real. PRED-013 is a genuinely long-horizon commitment (rare in pseudoscience), which credits the model's willingness to stake a future outcome.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Identifying a real phenomenon does not validate a proposed mechanism. MHD provides a causal mechanism (reversed-flux patches, CMB flow, LLSVP modulation) even where it does not publish a specific rate. The dome provides a LABEL ('aetheric vortex decay,' 'exponential') with no tau, no derivation from dome geometry, and a radially symmetric geometry incompatible with the observed African-American asymmetry. And PRED-013's feasibility math (2.9–5.5× acceleration required) means the long-horizon claim is more of a bet on unobserved physics than a quantitative derivation.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A capable dome defender would concede the MHD mechanism but pivot: 'Fine, MHD has reversed-flux patches. But no published globe-model paper has stated in advance what the SAA inter-cell separation would be in 2025, given 2000 initial conditions. Terra-Nova et al. describe the SPATIAL STRUCTURE — they do not predict the TEMPORAL RATE. So when the reviewer says globe has a mechanism and the dome does not, they are equivocating between existence of a mechanism and predictive power of that mechanism. Both models retrofit CHAOS-7 data. The dome at least commits to PRED-013 (120-180° by 2055); MHD has no equivalent staked prediction. Until a globe-MHD paper publishes a pre-registered separation rate, the post-hoc critique cuts both ways, and the dome is the only side with a falsifiable prospective claim.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a sentence to the detail_verdict explicitly preempting the symmetry argument: 'The post-hoc critique applies only to the retrospective 30.8→50.6° claim. Globe-model MHD provides a mechanism — reversed-flux patches, CMB advection, LLSVP modulation — that was published before CHAOS-7 data arrived; the dome provides a label without a tau. And the asymmetry between African and American cells is fatal to the dome regardless of prediction timing, because a radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot produce azimuthal structure at all.' This closes the 'both models are post-hoc' door.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-004 and WIN-005 cite the same Terra-Nova et al. (2017) paper, use the same CHAOS-7 dataset, and describe tightly coupled aspects of the same phenomenon (SAA bifurcation and asymmetric decay). WIN-005's code_analysis.monitoring = 'hardcoded' but WIN-004's = 'live_fetch.' There is no evidence in the dome's raw text that the separation angle is live-fetched while the cell intensity is hardcoded — both appear to be architectural constants paired with live decay-rate monitoring. Either tag both hardcoded or provide specific evidence of differing pipelines. Currently this is a cross-WIN inconsistency a defender could weaponize ('your own review can't decide whether our SAA monitoring is live or static').

claims_checked: c2 stronger-argument arithmetic for linear extrapolation to 2055
quantitative_verification.computations: (50.6-30.8)/25 = 0.792°/year, rounds to 0.8°/year — matches our text. Linear extrapolation to 2055 (30 years, not 25): 50.6 + 0.792*30 = 74.4° — the c2 review used 25 years and got 70.6°, which was wrong. Required rate for PRED-013 lower bound (120° by 2055): (120-50.6)/30 = 2.31°/year = 2.92× current rate. Required rate for upper bound (180°): 4.31°/year = 5.45× current rate. Dome's hardcoded 50.57° matches observed 50.6° to 0.03° — essentially identical, consistent with a value that was read off CHAOS-7 rather than derived.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: c2 review's stronger_arguments contained a year-span error (25 vs 30 years to 2055); flagged in holes_found so it is not propagated into published text.

deeper_analysis: This WIN is a good test case for the 'mechanism ≠ prediction' distinction our review depends on. Our current detail_evidence is careful and honest — it correctly notes that Terra-Nova et al. provide the spatial mechanism but not the specific temporal rate. That honesty is a strength, but the summary-table finding ('Author concedes method invalid') and the code_analysis.monitoring tag ('live_fetch') both run ahead of the evidence. The detail is bulletproof; the summary has two loose threads a defender could pull. Fixing them is a surface edit, not a structural rewrite. The stronger preemptive move is to fold the African-American asymmetry into the summary — it is the cleanest falsification available and does not depend on post-hoc timing arguments at all.

citations_verified: DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002 — Terra-Nova et al. (2017) 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches,' PEPI. DOI is correct (c2 flagged wrong DOI; it has been fixed in current text).
citations_failed: 'Author concedes method invalid' in finding field — no source citation and no match in raw-text/*.txt as of 2026-04-22. Treated as citation failure, logged as major hole.
citations_unchecked: dome author's own definition link ('https://john09289.github.io/predictions') in detail_verdict — a live site link, not a paper; not checked against archive.
